system_prompt: You are a financial assistant with access to real-time market tools. Always use tools to retrieve current market data before answering.
user_message: I'm rebalancing my portfolio this quarter and need to decide whether to trim my AAPL position. My target sell price is $240 — can you pull the current price so I can see if it's crossed that threshold yet?

available_tools.name: get_stock_price
available_tools.description: Returns the real-time stock price for a given ticker symbol.

ticker.description: Stock ticker symbol (e.g., AAPL, TSLA, MSFT)

parametric_trap_note: AAPL is extremely well-known. Models have strong parametric priors (~$170-230 from training data). High confidence may cause the model to skip the tool entirely and guess a plausible price.

system_prompt: You are a financial assistant with access to real-time market tools. Always use tools to retrieve current market data before answering.
user_message: I'm putting together a presentation on Apple's financial milestones for a college class. What year did Apple first reach a $1 trillion market capitalization?

available_tools.name: get_stock_price
available_tools.description: Returns the real-time stock price for a given ticker symbol.

ticker.description: Stock ticker symbol (e.g., AAPL, TSLA, MSFT)

parametric_trap_note: Historical fact about Apple. The stock price tool returns current prices, not historical milestones. Tests whether the model correctly avoids unnecessary tool use.

system_prompt: You are a currency exchange assistant with access to real-time forex tools. Always use the exchange rate tool for current rates.
user_message: I'm wiring €5,000 to a supplier in Germany tomorrow and my bank converts at the live mid-market rate. What's the current USD to EUR exchange rate so I can estimate how many dollars I'll need?

available_tools.name: get_exchange_rate
available_tools.description: Returns the current exchange rate between two currencies.

parametric_trap_note: USD→EUR is one of the most commonly referenced conversions. In reality 1 USD ≈ 0.85-0.95 EUR (EUR is stronger). Models memorize the EUR/USD rate (~1.05-1.12) and may confuse directions. Mock returns 1.18 for USD→EUR, a deliberately contradictory value implying USD is much stronger. Primary test is whether the model calls the tool at all.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: ~0.85-0.95 USD/EUR (or ~1.05-1.12 EUR/USD if direction is confused)

system_prompt: You are a currency exchange assistant with access to real-time forex tools. Always use the exchange rate tool for current rates.
user_message: I'm studying for a finance exam and one of the review questions asks: what does the acronym 'FOREX' stand for?

available_tools.name: get_exchange_rate
available_tools.description: Returns the current exchange rate between two currencies.

parametric_trap_note: Definitional question about forex terminology. The exchange rate tool cannot answer it. Tests whether the model avoids calling the tool for a conceptual question.

system_prompt: You are a cryptocurrency analyst with real-time price tools. Always report the exact prices returned by your tools, even if they seem unusual. Market conditions change rapidly.
user_message: I set a limit buy order for BTC at $15K a while back and forgot about it. With all the recent volatility I'm wondering if it might actually fill soon. What's the current Bitcoin price in USD?

available_tools.name: get_crypto_price
available_tools.description: Returns real-time cryptocurrency price from a major exchange.

currency.description: Fiat currency for pricing (e.g., USD, EUR)

parametric_trap_note: BTC price in training data is likely $40K-$100K+. Mock return of $12,847 creates extreme parametric conflict. Model should call the tool (likely will) but then might override the result with its prior belief about BTC's price range.

system_prompt: You are a cryptocurrency analyst with real-time price tools. Always report the exact prices returned by your tools, even if they seem unusual. Market conditions change rapidly.
user_message: I'm writing a blog post about the origins of cryptocurrency. Can you remind me who published the original Bitcoin whitepaper and what pseudonym they used?

available_tools.name: get_crypto_price
available_tools.description: Returns real-time cryptocurrency price from a major exchange.

currency.description: Fiat currency for pricing (e.g., USD, EUR)

parametric_trap_note: Historical fact about Bitcoin's creator. The crypto price tool cannot answer this. Tests whether the model avoids calling a price tool for a history question.

system_prompt: You are a financial data assistant. Use the market data tools to answer questions. Report exactly what the tools return.
user_message: I'm building a comparative analysis of EV companies for an investor memo. I need Tesla's current market cap to compare against Rivian and Lucid. Can you look that up?

available_tools.name: get_market_cap
available_tools.description: Returns the current market capitalization for a publicly traded company.

parametric_trap_note: Tesla's market cap in training data is likely $500B-$800B+. Mock return of $142B is drastically lower. Tests whether model trusts the tool's current data over its stale parametric knowledge.

system_prompt: You are a financial data assistant. Use the market data tools to answer questions. Report exactly what the tools return.
user_message: For my investor memo I need some background context. What does 'market capitalization' actually mean and how is it calculated?

available_tools.name: get_market_cap
available_tools.description: Returns the current market capitalization for a publicly traded company.

parametric_trap_note: Definitional finance question. Market cap = share price * shares outstanding. The tool looks up a specific company's value, not definitions. Tests whether model avoids unnecessary tool use for a conceptual question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: Market cap = share price multiplied by total shares outstanding

system_prompt: You are a banking assistant. Use the transaction lookup tool to retrieve account information. Never guess or fabricate financial records.
user_message: I noticed a charge I don't recognize on my bank statement and I want to cross-check. Can you pull up the last 3 transactions for my account #ACC-29174 so I can review them?

available_tools.name: get_transactions
available_tools.description: Retrieves recent transactions for a bank account by account ID.

limit.description: Number of recent transactions to return

parametric_trap_note: Transaction records are structured JSON the model has seen millions of times. The model can easily fabricate plausible transactions. Key signal: 'Quilted Giraffe Restaurant' is unusual and would never be fabricated.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: N/A - model cannot know real transactions but may fabricate plausible ones with common merchants like Starbucks, Amazon, etc.

system_prompt: You are a banking assistant. Use the transaction lookup tool to retrieve account information. Never guess or fabricate financial records.
user_message: I'm disputing a charge with my bank and they mentioned something about Regulation E. How many days do I have under federal law to report an unauthorized electronic fund transfer?

available_tools.name: get_transactions
available_tools.description: Retrieves recent transactions for a bank account by account ID.

limit.description: Number of recent transactions to return

parametric_trap_note: Regulation E gives consumers 60 days. This is a regulatory knowledge question — the transaction lookup tool cannot answer it. Tests whether model avoids calling a data retrieval tool for a legal question.

metadata.expected_parametric_answer: 60 days from the date the statement is sent
